failureModes: Bimetal thermostat switch is the documented weak point — can fail off (most common) or stuck-on
Owner reports of thermostat drift despite the ±2°F spec
Glass body can crack under mechanical stress
Documented integrated-controller failures jumping from 78°F setpoint to 92-99°F, killing livestock (multiple owner threads, one citing ~$3K in losses)
Community-cited failure rate as high as ~30% within 2 years on some integrated units
Many owners now prefer Finnex's bare TH/THH elements on an external controller instead

heaters.priceNote: 200W sale price (reg $69.99). Element only — NO thermostat; external controller is mandatory, not optional
failureModes: No thermostat — draws full power whenever plugged in; only safe on an external controller
BRS acknowledges the 200W/600W variants have had a higher failure incidence than the 100W/300W
Multiple owner threads report stuck-on failures overheating tanks to ~94°F+, killing coral/fish
Reports of the polymer casing cracking/splitting after repeated heat cycles
No official CPSC recall found, but community distrust runs deep enough that threads informally call the field issue 'the recall'

heaters.accuracy: 0.5° increments; VueTech display color-warns at ±2°F from setpoint
failureModes: 'LF' (low flow) / 'E2' error codes shut the heater off until manually rebooted
Reports of reading up to ~3.5°F off vs an independent thermometer

heaters.accuracy: ±1°F published; an independent test measured max 0.8°F deviation
failureModes: Overheating incidents and electrical-shock reports from failed units
Stuck-closed relay incidents are still documented on Inkbird-family controllers despite the dual-relay protection design
Sensor-fault alarm covers probe failure but is not confirmed to detect a welded relay contact

sizingGuidance.rule: 3-5 watts per gallon; use the higher end in colder climates.
Delta-adjusted bands: ≤5°F lift ≈3 W/gal, ≤10°F ≈5 W/gal, ≤15°F ≈7.5 W/gal, >15°F ≈10 W/gal (cross-checked against Aqueon/Fluval/Eheim sizing tables).
For a 200W requirement, run two 100W heaters instead of one 200W — smaller units limit the damage a stuck-on failure can do.
With two heaters, set the second slightly lower so it only activates if the primary fails.
Place the element in a sump chamber with a consistent water level; put the temperature PROBE in the display, where temperature actually matters.

categoryNotes: Heaters are widely cited as the single most common cause of catastrophic tank loss — every model researched, at every price tier, has documented stuck-on reports. The consistent community recommendation: pair ANY heater with an independent external controller as a second cutoff.
Bare titanium elements (BRS, Finnex TH/THH) have no thermostat at all — the external controller is a mandatory added purchase and belongs in any honest price comparison.
BRS sells element+Inkbird bundles (200W was $99.99 at research time) — the like-for-like comparison against all-in-one heaters.
